---
title: 'Cold'
date: '2014-02-11T13:46:00.0030000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'It''s Author Fun'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5401365907329072876'
blogger_filename: '/2014/02/cold.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/It_s Author Fun/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Writing Practice'
---

<h3 style="text-align: center;">
02/11/2014 Writing Practice</h3>
The ground is wet. Cold drizzle surrounds everything. Droplets of water blanket the car. So he opens the door and sits down to start the engine. He loves this weather. There is something about cold that makes him feel very present and alive. He turns on the wipers to clean the window and the water doesn't leave, it doesn't move. "It can't be THAT cold out here can it?" Sure enough. Those droplets are ice. Should have left a few minutes earlier today.<br />
<br />
****<br />
<br />
Thank you for reading. I hope you enjoyed this. I'd love to make a community with this project. If you would like to add, comment on, critique, or otherwise participate... that would be great! Please include any information about yourself, your blog/wesbite/amazon author page that you want to share with the community also.<br />
<br />
<br />
<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">It's Author Fun</a>&nbsp;By&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/DarrellWolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe</a><br />
<br />
To Subscribe to the&nbsp;<a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a><br />
<br />
For Non-Fiction writing by&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell G. Wolfe: Click Here</a><br />
<br />
Any work not otherwise attributed is Copy Right Darrell G. Wolfe<br />
<br />
All Rights Reserved Â© Darrell G. Wolfe<br />
<br />
<b>Disclosure of Material Connection: Some of the links in the post above are â€œaffiliate links.â€ This means if you click on the link and purchase the item, I will receive an affiliate commission. I may also have received one or more of the products or services mentioned above for free in the hope that I would mention it on my blog. Regardless, I only recommend products or services I use personally and believe will add value to my readers. I am disclosing this in accordance with the Federal Trade Commissionâ€™s&nbsp;<a href="http://www.gpo.gov/fdsys/pkg/CFR-2003-title16-vol1/content-detail.html" target="_blank">16 CFR, Part 255</a>: â€œGuides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising.â€ A Big Thank You to&nbsp;<a href="http://michaelhyatt.com/five-ways-to-comply-with-the-new-ftc-guidelines-for-bloggers.html" target="_blank">Michael Hyatt</a>&nbsp;for helping us Bloggers with these new legal disclosure requirements.</b><br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<div style="clear: both; float: left; padding-bottom: 10px; text-align: right;">
<a href="https://www.facebook.com/DarrellGWolfe" imageanchor="1"><img alt="Follow me on Facebook" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-icon-8bd715bb0a75.png" style="border: 0px; height: 32px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 32px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="https://twitter.com/DarrellWolfe"><img alt="Follow  &lt;b&gt;YOUR-TWITTER-ACCOUNT-NAME&lt;/b&gt; on Twitter" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/twitter-logo-bb131234195a.png" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://www.youtube.com/user/darrellgwolfe"><img alt="Follow YOUR-CHANNEL-ID on YouTube" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/youtube-icon-bea5452f6261.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml"><img alt="RSS subscription icon" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/rss-logo-a37a894526ed.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br /></div>
